observation: The public website captures a tour request, then depends on staff to notice the submission, respond and coordinate the next step manually.

audit.title: Inquiry capture
audit.note: Families have a visible way to raise their hand.

audit.title: Immediate acknowledgement
audit.note: No public indication confirms what happens immediately after submission.

audit.title: Tour scheduling
audit.note: No self-scheduling path is visible after the request.

audit.title: Follow-up visibility
audit.note: The public journey does not show a structured follow-up sequence.

firstResponse:
Hi [First name],

Thank you for reaching out to Sample Senior Living Community. We received your request and would be happy to help with the next step.

You can choose a convenient tour time here: [Tour link]

If you would rather speak with someone first, reply to this message and a member of our team will follow up.
smsResponse: Hi [First name], this is Sample Senior Living Community. We received your tour request. Choose a convenient time here: [Tour link]. Reply here if you would rather speak with our team first.

followUps.subject: Still considering a visit?
followUps.body: Hi [First name], I wanted to make the next step easy. Would you like help choosing a tour time, or would a quick call be better?

followUps.subject: A quick question
followUps.body: Hi [First name], what matters most as you compare communities: care, location, timing or something else? Reply with one word and we will point you in the right direction.

followUps.subject: Keep this open?
followUps.body: Hi [First name], should we keep your tour request open, or would you prefer that we close the loop for now? Either answer is completely fine.

metrics.name: Response coverage
metrics.detail: Eligible inquiries receiving the approved acknowledgement

metrics.name: Time to next step
metrics.detail: Time between inquiry and a clear route forward

metrics.name: Tours scheduled
metrics.detail: Inquiries selecting an approved visit time
